prompt:
Create a clean black-line-art medical-illustration in the universal patient-information-leaflet style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on pure white background, no color, no frame, no border, no captions, no labels other than the embossed text on the tube itself, no logos, no numbers, no extra elements.

=== SUBJECT ===
A single standalone illustration of the ASSEMBLED PROTECTOR TUBE shown STANDING UPRIGHT (cap on top, body on bottom) in a 1:1 square composition, centered, with generous clean white margins. NO hands, NO vial, NO arrow, NO bag, NO other objects. Just the assembled protector tube as a clean line-art product illustration.

=== TUBE — MUST MATCH THE PROVIDED REFERENCE IMAGE EXACTLY ===
The provided reference image (protector tube top.png) shows the real assembled protector tube laid on its side. Your output must be a faithful line-art replica of THIS PHOTO when rotated 90° so the tube stands upright with cap-on-top.

Key features to replicate from the photo:

  • OVERALL PROPORTIONS: STUBBY and CHUNKY. Total assembled height ≈ 1.3x the tube's diameter — VERY SHORT relative to width. The tube is wider than it is tall feeling, NOT a tall slim test tube. Picture a stack of two short hockey pucks of slightly different heights.

  • BLACK CAP (top section): a short squat solid-black cylinder. Cap height ≈ 0.5x its diameter (much wider than tall). Solid black fill. Sits on top of the body with a clean horizontal seam line.

  • WHITE BODY (bottom section): a stubby cylinder of the SAME WIDE DIAMETER as the cap. Body height ≈ 0.8–0.9x its diameter (slightly taller than the cap but still stubby — wider than tall). White (line-art outline only, no fill). Sits flat on the ground in the composition.

  • CAP and BODY share the SAME WIDE DIAMETER. Together they form one continuous chunky cylinder.

=== EMBOSSED TEXT — REPLICATE THE PHOTO'S TEXT ORIENTATION EXACTLY ===

In the reference photo, the embossed text runs ALONG the tube's length axis (like text on a book spine). When the tube is stood up:

  • BLACK CAP: the word 'DIALED' is embossed running ALONG the cap's vertical length axis. Each letter is rotated 90° from upright (the letters lie sideways with their tops facing one side of the tube and their bottoms facing the other side — exactly like text on a book spine). The FIRST D sits at the very TOP of the cap (the cap-end, farthest from the body). The letters proceed downward: I, A, L, E, and the final D at the BOTTOM of the cap (at the cap/body seam). Reading top-down (tilting head 90°): D-I-A-L-E-D forward. Letters are thin WHITE outline strokes against the solid-black cap fill (line-art embossed look — letter interior remains black, NOT solid white blocks). Bold sans-serif capital letterforms.

  • WHITE BODY: the word 'HEALTH' is embossed running ALONG the body's vertical length axis. Each letter is rotated 90° from upright (sideways, book-spine style). The FIRST H sits at the very TOP of the body (just below the cap/body seam). The letters proceed downward: E, A, L, T, and the final H at the BOTTOM of the body (near its base). Reading top-down (tilting head 90°): H-E-A-L-T-H forward. Letters are thin BLACK outline strokes against the white body (line-art embossed look — letter interior remains white, NOT solid black blocks). Bold sans-serif capital letterforms.

  • Combined reading top-to-bottom of the upright tube: DIALED (cap) → HEALTH (body) = 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly top-down, both words correctly spelled forward, all letters in the same sideways orientation as the photo reference.

=== STYLE ===
Uniform medium-weight black ink line work on pure white background. Minimal short parallel hatching only where needed for soft shadow / volume on the tube's curved surfaces. No other color. 1:1 square composition. Subject centered with generous clean white margins around it. Drawn-by-hand line work, clean and crisp.

No frame, no border, no caption, no labels other than the embossed DIALED on the cap and HEALTH on the body, no logos, no numbers, no hands, no other objects.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
